## 1. The Compression Hierarchy {#sec-compression-hierarchy}

<!-- STATUS: Stub — to be written.

Purpose: Establish the central conceptual distinction. Short, decisive.

Core claims:
- Data → information → knowledge as progressively deeper compression.
- Three levels precisely distinguished:
  - Algorithmic compression: reduces bits. Surface redundancy. Does not generalise.
  - Organisational compression: discovers structure. Encodes shape rather than
    instances. Generalises (grammar, taxonomy, chess principles vs memorised games).
  - Semantic compression: builds navigable meaning geometry. Supports inference
    through structure. This is what transformers do.
- Rate-distortion theory introduced as the natural formalism: each level is a
  rate-distortion problem with progressively more abstract relevance criterion.
  What counts as "loss" changes at each level — this is what makes the hierarchy
  a hierarchy. Cite Shannon 1959 (distortion measure as choice).
- The hierarchy is not substrate-specific. Flagged here, developed in Ch 4.

Target length: ~35% shorter than v1 Section 1. The taxonomy is clear within
three paragraphs. Do not over-elaborate.

Key citations: @shannon_1959, @achille_soatto_2018, @tishby_zaslavsky_2015

Voice and level: match Chapters 2, 3, 5. First idea, first sentence. Trust
the reader.
-->
